action.reasoning: Observation: The current model (SimplifiedMLP) achieved a mean R² of -1371.20, which is worse than our best model from iteration 10 that achieved -526.47. The main issue continues to be the poor humidity prediction (R² of -3165.05). The model architecture and training from iteration 10 had better performance, so I need to go back to that approach. Analysis: Our best performance came from the model in iteration 10 with the weighted R2 loss function. The key elements were: 1) A specialized architecture with separate heads for different output types, 2) A weighted R2 loss that prioritized poorly performing components like humidity, 3) Properly sized output dimensions (60 temp + 60 humid + 8 radiation/precip = 128). Decision: I'll revert to the architecture from iteration 10 and enhance it specifically for humidity prediction by increasing the capacity of the humidity head and adjusting the loss weights.
